basics.name: Ivan Uhalin
basics.label: Mobile Developer
basics.summary:
The person from the world of science and technology. Programming is a huge part of me — I live by dreaming up ideas, taking care of the quality of my code. I started my professional career as a Frontend Developer but after a few years I switched to Mobile Developer.
In my free time, I listen to music, listen to podcasts on the most unusual topics, explore and observe open source community, read articles on technical topics, watch films and much more.

work.name: Pochta Tech
work.summary: Development with a team of an internal service for company engineers to view system messages in NATS streaming. The backend team worked with .NET and NATS CLI, while the frontend team worked with React, React Router and the internal components library. Jira was used for task management and GitLab was used for source code management. At first, I spend more time communicating with the customer and PM, as well as co-mentoring the team.

work.name: Freelance
work.summary: Refactoring, merging two SPAs based on React, React Router, MUI, Mobx, Highcharts responsible for the creation of company reports.

work.name: OOO "SK NEBO"
work.summary: Integration of SPAs written in the Vue ecosystem (Vuex, Vue Route) into the CRM interface. Ant Design components were used to create the application interface and DevExtreme with Highcharts to illustrate the statistics. Along with application development, it was necessary to maintain and develop the company's business processes.

work.name: Freelance
work.summary: Development of an auto auction site for integration with a CRM using vanilla tools such as JavaScript, HTML, CSS using the BEM methodology.

work.name: Self-employed
work.summary: First steps in programming. It was necessary to write a social network bot to sell food in a closed area. As a result, the bot was written in JavaScript and hosted on Heroku as a NodeJS application. At the time, I had no idea how REST APIs and databases work, so I developed my bicycle: I used the service that parses Google Documents into JSON and allows me to access it as a REST API endpoint. In addition to programming, I had to deal with delivery management, promotion, design, etc.
